name: Austria
contextBlurb:
As of 2025, Austria applies the EU MiCA regime. Stablecoins are classified as asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) and e-money tokens (EMTs). The FMA is the national competent authority. ART issuers need MiCA authorisation; EMTs may only be issued by banks or e-money institutions. Holders benefit from MiCA safeguards: reserves/custody and disclosures, and redemption rights (EMTs at par; ARTs per MiCA terms).
Tax: crypto income is taxed at 27.5% (from 1 Mar 2022, with a transitional rule for assets acquired after 28 Feb 2021).

legislation.title: Apr 2026 ESMA confirmed that the MiCA transitional period ends mid-2026; crypto firms must hold a full MiCA/CASP licence by that deadline. As of April 2026, 199 CASPs are licensed across the EU; no Asset-Referenced Token (ART) issuer has yet been approved.
Nine European banks—including Austria’s Raiffeisen—announce a consortium to launch a MiCAR-compliant euro stablecoin.
AMF, Austria’s FMA and Italy’s Consob call for a stronger EU supervisory framework under MiCA.
ECB blog outlines financial-stability and sovereignty risks from stablecoins and options for Europe.
Austria’s FMA grants MiCA (CASP) authorisation to Bybit EU GmbH, permitting multiple crypto-asset services under Article 63 MiCAR.

news.headline: Digital Watch – ESMA confirms end of MiCA transition period (April 2026)

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn:
As of 2025, Austria applies the EU MiCA regime. Stablecoins are classified as asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) and e-money tokens (EMTs). The FMA is the national competent authority. ART issuers need MiCA authorisation; EMTs may only be issued by banks or e-money institutions. Holders benefit from MiCA safeguards: reserves/custody and disclosures, and redemption rights (EMTs at par; ARTs per MiCA terms).
Tax: crypto income is taxed at 27.5% (from 1 Mar 2022, with a transitional rule for assets acquired after 28 Feb 2021).
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Under the EU MiCA regime, Austria classifies stablecoins as e-money tokens (EMTs) and asset-referenced tokens (ARTs). EMTs reference a single official currency and are redeemable at par in funds; only credit institutions or e-money institutions may issue them. ARTs aim to keep a stable value by refe

regulators.name: Austrian Financial Market Authority
regulators.role: Designated national competent authority under MiCA-VVG. Authorises and supervises ART issuers and crypto-asset service providers (CASPs); EMTs are supervised under banking/e-money frameworks. The FMA

regulators.name: Austrian National Bank
regulators.role: Austria’s central bank. Monitors financial stability and supports macroprudential policy (incl. via the FMSB) and conducts payment systems oversight; collaborates with the FMA on systemic-risk issues.
